verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific single-pass transmembrane protein (Ig-like / IZUMO family) that resides in the acrosomal membrane in non-capacitated sperm. Upon capacitation and acrosomal reaction, IZUMO1 redistributes to the entire sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet, where it directly participates in sperm-egg fusion. It has its own TM anchor and reaches the outer leaflet, but this surface display is restricted to the narrow germline/gamete (sperm) lineage and is activation-dependent. This maps to contextual / tissue_restricted_surface: the protein body does reach the outer leaflet via its own TM anchor, but exclusively in a single, narrow developmental/germline cell type.
